instructions_process_image_prompt: Single vertical instructions-only recipe process collage divided into 6 equal panels in a 2-column 3-row grid, each panel numbered 1 to 6 with a small bold number in the top corner (numbers only), all panels locked to identical soft warm natural window light with warm golden highlights, a bright appetizing pastel tone keyed to a creamy-golden palette, and a neutral white marble background. Show only cooking-method steps from prep through the last active step before completion; never show serving, plating, finished dish presentation, or the final product. Panel 1 (no hands): Mise en place on white marble—small glass prep bowls (2 oz and 4 oz) holding chopped aromatics and seasonings, a medium stainless mixing bowl (3 qt), a silicone spatula, a chef’s knife, measuring spoons, and a 10-inch cast-iron skillet beside a parchment-lined sheet pan; ingredients look raw and separated, with a light scatter of salt grains and a few herb flecks on the marble. Panel 2 (one active hand): First active technique—RIGHT hand whisking a pale creamy mixture in a 3-qt stainless bowl with a stainless balloon whisk; mixture shows streaks and small lumps mid-whisk, a few splatters on the bowl rim; shallow depth of field focused on the whisk trails, background softly blurred. Panel 3 (no hands): Passive stage—mixture resting in the same bowl for a brief set, surface smoothing with a few bubbles and a thin glossy sheen; a light dusting of flour-like powder and a faint smear on the marble nearby; no hands visible. Panel 4 (one active hand; second hand only to steady if needed): Second active combining—LEFT hand folding in a chunky component with a silicone spatula in a 2. 5-qt ceramic mixing bowl (off-white), optional RIGHT hand lightly steadying the bowl edge; visible chunks and streaks, imperfect folds, bits clinging to the spatula; shallow depth of field on the fold motion. Panel 5 (minimal hand visible): Transfer/pour—minimal fingertips visible on the rim of a clear glass measuring jug (2-cup) as the mixture is poured into a 10-inch cast-iron skillet (or 9x13-inch matte metal baking dish) on the marble; thick ribbons and drips, uneven pooling, a few drops on the skillet edge; shallow depth of field on the pour stream. Panel 6 (no hands): Final pre-completion step—skillet/dish positioned on the center rack of an open oven, warm oven glow, dish just about to bake; no hands visible; a few crumbs on the oven rack and a slightly messy rim for lived-in realism.
